name: Kashi Vishwanath Temple
description: One of the 12 Jyotirlingas dedicated to Lord Shiva. The most sacred Hindu temple in Varanasi, located on the western bank of the holy Ganga river.

name: Dashashwamedh Ghat
description: The main ghat in Varanasi, famous for the spectacular Ganga Aarti ceremony held every evening with large fire lamps.

name: Manikarnika Ghat
description: The most sacred cremation ghat. Hindus believe cremation here leads to moksha. The eternal fire has burned for thousands of years.

name: Sarnath
description: Where Lord Buddha gave his first sermon. Home to the Dhamekh Stupa, Ashoka Pillar, and Sarnath Museum with the Lion Capital.

name: Assi Ghat
description: The southernmost ghat of Varanasi. Popular for morning yoga, meditation, and the subah-e-banaras cultural ceremony.

name: Tulsi Manas Temple
description: Modern marble temple built in 1964, dedicated to Lord Ram. Named after poet Tulsidas who wrote Ramcharitmanas here.

name: Durga Temple
description: 18th-century temple dedicated to Goddess Durga with Nagara-style architecture and vibrant red walls. Also called the Monkey Temple.

name: Sankat Mochan Hanuman Temple
description: One of the most revered Hanuman temples in India, founded by Tulsidas. Famous for Tuesday/Saturday worship and laddoo prasad.

name: Ramnagar Fort
description: 18th-century Mughal-era sandstone fort on the eastern bank of the Ganges. Houses vintage cars, royal costumes, and ancient weaponry.

name: Bharat Mata Temple
description: Unique temple dedicated to Mother India with a large marble relief map of undivided India. Inaugurated by Mahatma Gandhi in 1936.

name: Dhamekh Stupa
description: Massive 43-meter stupa at Sarnath built around 500 AD, marking where Buddha delivered his first sermon.

name: Annapurna Temple
description: Temple dedicated to Goddess Annapurna, deity of food. Near Kashi Vishwanath, features a gold-plated idol.

name: Gyanvapi Mosque
description: 17th-century mosque built by Aurangzeb, adjacent to Kashi Vishwanath Temple. Represents Varanasi's composite heritage.

name: Sarnath Museum
description: Houses the original Lion Capital of Ashoka — India's national emblem. Contains Buddhist artifacts from the 3rd century BC.

name: Kabir Math
description: Ashram of Sant Kabir, the 15th-century mystic poet who preached unity among religions. Contains his original loom.

name: Kedar Ghat
description: A quiet, colorful ghat with Kedareshwar Temple. Popular among South Indian pilgrims for its vibrant painted steps.

name: Panchganga Ghat
description: Sacred ghat where five rivers are believed to converge. Spectacularly lit with diyas during Dev Deepawali festival.

name: Alamgir Mosque
description: 17th-century mosque by Aurangzeb overlooking Panchganga Ghat, blending Hindu and Islamic architectural styles.

name: Chaukhandi Stupa
description: Ancient stupa marking where Buddha met his first five disciples. Features a unique octagonal Mughal tower on top.

name: Nepali Temple
description: Pagoda-style wooden temple at Lalita Ghat built by the King of Nepal with exquisite wood carvings.
